meaning: n.节日,盛宴,筵席,宴会,酒席vt.盛宴,款待,享乐,请客vi.参加宴会,享受
examples: The Jewish feast of Passover began last night.
The feast was served by his mother and sisters.
They were happy to partake of our feast, but not to share our company.
Dates usually kick off the feast, since the sugar- and vitamin-rich fruit helps your stomach readjust to eating.
The iconic building opened in 1869 and is a visual feast with sumptuous interiors.
